financial.note: HANA maintains a robust 'Net Cash' position with very low debt-to-equity; expansion is largely self-funded from retained earnings.
Provides working capital and letters of credit for international trade; sensitive to floating interest rates on current liabilities.

supply_chain.note: 11.5 billion THB JV to build Thailand's first Silicon Carbide (SiC) wafer plant, diversifying into the high-growth EV supply chain.
Selected as a primary supplier for solid-state cooling systems starting 3Q26, expanding HANA's footprint in AI and data center tech.
Primary buyers for PCBA and HDD-related IC assembly; performance is tied to the global storage and PC refresh cycles.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Core R&D and manufacturing hub for SiC technology, pivotal for the group's 2026-2027 power electronics pivot.
Strategic relocation site for production shifted out of China due to US-China geopolitical trade fragmentation.

competitors.note: Direct rival for automotive and industrial electronic manufacturing market share in Thailand.
Competes in the global EMS (Electronic Manufacturing Services) space for specialized industrial components.
Rivalry in high-volume PCBA and consumer electronics manufacturing segments.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a 100% exporter, THB depreciation significantly boosts THB-denominated revenue and operating margins.
Gold is a critical raw material for IC bonding wires; price spikes directly increase Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).
Major component for PCB substrates and lead frames; high copper prices squeeze manufacturing spreads.
Recovery in 2026 driven by AI adoption and EV demand directly correlates with factory utilization rates.
